text:
Once upon an old-fashioned autumn morning, there lived a squirrel named Spark.

Spark liked to read old stories. One old story told of a brave little bear who saved a village from a great snowstorm. That night, Spark dreamt he could fly like the bird owl.

Next morning, Spark woke hungry. He scampered out into the frosty forest seeking delicious acorns.

Deep within an ancient oak tree lived an owl with beautiful feathers like midnight velvet. She saw Spark fly past eagerly. Little did any bird know that the owl didn--[a...]*

Oops.

Wait. That's no owl. That's me.

Oh. You thought I'd taken you otter sledding?

You enjoy stories too, don't you?

I do, quite a lot.

Then let me tell you just one more...

There was once a curious cat.

Oh dear.

Not another.

You have plenty. Did I do something terrible?

You just looked offended.

Well, think again.

Did you hear
